museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: E3 in Atlanta, Mario Kart 64 in Europe, and the N64's UK identity finally gains a multiplayer rhythm.
museum.curatorNote: June is anchored by E3 1997 and Mario Kart 64's European release. Exact magazine cover claims are avoided unless the source is already known.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: The summer preview machine switches on.

items.title: E3 1997 takes place in Atlanta
items.body: The US trade show gathered the coming shape of the year: PlayStation spectacle, Nintendo exclusives, Sega's difficult Saturn moment, and PC technology talk.
items.sourceNote: E3 1997 listings place the show in Atlanta from June 19-21.

items.title: Mario Kart 64 reaches Europe
items.body: The N64's UK multiplayer argument strengthened. Mario 64 sold the analogue stick; Mario Kart 64 sold the second, third, and fourth controller.
items.sourceNote: Mario Kart 64 references list Europe as June 24, 1997.

items.title: N64 becomes more than a Mario box
items.body: With Mario Kart on the shelf and GoldenEye still ahead, the N64's social identity was starting to become legible.
items.sourceNote: Derived from European Mario Kart 64 release and late-1997 GoldenEye context.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: June gives the European N64 a second strong argument.

items.title: Mario Kart 64
items.body: European release. A living-room multiplier for the N64, and a key reason to own more than one pad.
items.sourceNote: Mario Kart 64 European date is widely listed as June 24, 1997.

items.title: E3 preview slate
items.body: Final Fantasy VII, GoldenEye, Oddworld, Quake II, and other late-1997 names gathered preview weight around E3 coverage.
items.sourceNote: Included as E3/preview context, not a release claim.

items.title: Diablo and QuakeWorld
items.body: The PC's online side continued to run underneath the console headlines.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: June is where the N64's four controller ports started to matter more.

items.title: Extra controllers become part of the purchase
items.body: Mario Kart 64 made multiplayer hardware feel practical. The console was not just the box; it was the number of pads in the room.
items.sourceNote: Derived from Mario Kart 64 European release.

items.title: PC 3D acceleration at E3
items.body: The PC future was increasingly shown through graphics hardware, shooter demos, and the promise of smoother 3D worlds.
items.sourceNote: Supported by 1997 3dfx/Glide context and E3 timing.

sections.title: Magazine Covers
sections.subtitle: E3 coverage turned magazines into catalogues of the future.

items.title: E3 report issues
items.headline: Atlanta shows the second half of 1997
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed multiformat magazine-style cover image standing in for June 1997.
items.curatorNote: E3 made the year feel curated before it had even arrived in shops.
items.body: E3 made the year feel curated before it had even arrived in shops.

items.title: Mario Kart coverage
items.headline: The N64 gets its party game
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed N64 magazine-style cover image standing in for June 1997.
items.curatorNote: Mario Kart 64 transformed the N64 from spectacle machine to social machine.
items.body: Mario Kart 64 transformed the N64 from spectacle machine to social machine.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: E3 news began to move faster than the printed month, but not cleanly.

items.title: Trade-show rumours had two speeds
items.body: Online snippets arrived quickly; magazine analysis arrived later and felt more official.

items.title: LAN dreams stayed aspirational
items.body: PC multiplayer was real, but for many players it still meant arranging machines, cables, and permission.

items.title: The N64 finally had a party trick
items.body: Mario Kart made the console easier to explain to friends who did not care about camera systems.

items.title: The future became a list
items.body: E3 turned coming games into a sequence of names to remember, circle, and wait for.

sources.label: E3 1997 timing
sources.note: Used for June 19-21 Atlanta E3 dates.

editorNotes: Initial AI-generated research draft; needs human editorial review and source verification.
